product: Legacy Compute Preview System Schema Deprecation
tagline: Transitioning to the new system.compute schema for enhanced data management.
what_it_does: The legacy `compute_preview` system schema has been deprecated and will be removed from the `system` catalog, urging users to transition to the `system.compute` schema. This change is part of Databricks' ongoing efforts to streamline system table management and improve operational data accessibility. System tables are utilized for monitoring costs, auditing security events, and tracking compute performance across the Databricks environment.
why_it_matters: This update is crucial for maintaining an organized and efficient data management system, reducing potential confusion from deprecated schemas. By consolidating to the `system.compute` schema, organizations can enhance their operational oversight, ensuring better governance and compliance while optimizing resource usage.
plain_summary: The legacy compute preview system schema has been deprecated and will be removed from the system catalog, urging users to transition to the system.compute schema. This change is part of Databricks' ongoing efforts to streamline system table management and…

capabilities.title: Enhanced Data Monitoring
capabilities.desc: Utilize system tables to monitor costs and performance metrics effectively.

capabilities.title: Improved Security Auditing
capabilities.desc: Audit security events through accessible system tables for better compliance.

capabilities.title: Streamlined Schema Management
capabilities.desc: Transitioning to a single schema simplifies data management and reduces errors.

capabilities.title: Historical Observability
capabilities.desc: Access historical data for comprehensive insights into operational activities.

prerequisites: Workspace must be enabled for Unity Catalog.
Metastore must be on Unity Catalog Privilege Model Version 1.0.
use_cases: Monitoring compute resource usage over time.
Auditing and tracking security events across workspaces.
Analyzing billing and usage data for cost optimization.
limitations: Only a subset of system tables is available in AWS GovCloud.
System tables are read-only and cannot be modified.

steps.title: Identify Legacy Schema Usage
steps.desc: Review existing queries and applications that utilize the `compute_preview` schema.

steps.title: Update Queries
steps.desc: Modify queries to reference the new `system.compute` schema.

steps.title: Test Functionality
steps.desc: Ensure that all updated queries function correctly with the new schema.

steps.title: Monitor Performance
steps.desc: Track performance metrics post-transition to ensure no degradation.

steps.title: Decommission Legacy Schema
steps.desc: Remove references to the `compute_preview` schema once all dependencies are updated.

key_takeaway: Transitioning from the deprecated `compute_preview` schema to `system.compute` enhances data management and operational efficiency.
